calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-30-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Choi, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel:415.788.1900, for defendants and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF CHARLES SCHWAB'S Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs as follows:

Plaintiff Charles R. Schwab Jr.'s motion to strike or tax costs is granted in part, and defendants Royse Law Firm, P.C. and Roger Royse are awarded $8,991.65 in costs.

Defendants are entitled to recover the following statutory costs, in the total amount of $8,991.65:
-Filing and motion fees ($1,540.00) (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1033.5(a)(1));
-Jury fees ($330.00) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(1));
-Expenses of taking, video recording, and transcribing necessary depositions ($6,138.60) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(3)). Plaintiff, having failed to prevail in this case on summary judgment, cannot be heard after the fact to complain that depositions that Defendants took were not "necessary." (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 132 [need for deposition is determined from the pretrial vantage point of a litigant; challenging party bears the burden the prove the costs unnecessary at the time of incurring it or unreasonable].)
-Service of process ($983.05) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(4)).

Because defendants did not file a declaration in opposition to the motion to tax or strike, they have not met their burden to show that the nonstatutory costs claimed in the memorandum of costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation or reasonable in amount. (Sec. 1033.5(c)(2),(4); see Nelson v. Anderson, 72 Cal.App.4th at 132.) Accordingly, the motion to strike those costs is granted.

Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Hearing is held and the Court makes the following ruling:
